page: Microsoft Azure E16s_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Esv7 server family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz, featuring 8 physical cores and 16 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled. The processor is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing users to attach external persistent storage volumes. The virtual machine runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not feature any integrated GPU hardware or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the E16s_v7 in the average tier for most compute and memory operations, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2154.98 and a multi-core score of 15978.65. Large-block memory bandwidth reaches 133.34 GB/sec for read operations and 68.36 GB/sec for write operations. Notably, single-threaded gzip compression performs in the top tier at 35.26 GB/sec. In database and key-value store tests, the server handles 26.17 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 6966.31 on PassMark database operations. For lightweight machine learning tasks, it achieves a strong text generation speed of 319.32 tokens per second using a small 135M LLM model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E16s_v7 provides a high-density memory configuration relative to its 16 vCPUs, making it a viable choice for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of excess compute cores. The absence of bundled local storage represents a tradeoff, requiring external storage provisioning. This server is suited for relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, enterprise applications, and moderate data processing pipelines.
description: The Microsoft Azure E16s_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv7 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a substantial 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio. While it lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark results show average-tier compute and memory performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 15978.65 and large-block memory read bandwidth of 133.34 GB/sec. It excels in single-threaded gzip compression, reaching top-tier speeds of 35.26 GB/sec. This instance is well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory caching with Redis, and enterprise application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, designed for database workloads and in-memory caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16s_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized VM with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, running on Hyper-V.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring a high-frequency Intel Xeon processor and a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz
16 vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression performance at 35.26 GB/sec
Best suited for relational databases, Redis caching, and memory-bound enterprise applications
